key_themes: Nuclear sector rally driven by White House space nuclear mandate and AI/data center demand
NRC approvals, strategic partnerships (Framatome, Oak Ridge, Ebara), and technology milestones
Strong $1.3B liquidity position providing development runway
Class action lawsuit overhang related to ENTRA1 partnership and Fluor share sales
Commercialization risks, potential deployment delays, but bullish analyst targets (~$20+ avg)
summary: NuScale Power (SMR) has surged over 40% this week to around $12.78, participating in a broad nuclear sector rally sparked by White House support for space nuclear power (lunar bases, Mars missions) and persistent AI/data center electricity demand. The company highlights NRC design approval for its uprated 77 MWe module, multiple partnerships, and $1.3 billion in liquidity following ATM raises, positioning it as the leading publicly traded SMR pure-play despite ongoing losses. While a securities class action lawsuit over alleged misleading statements regarding partner ENTRA1's experience (deadline around April 20, 2026) and prior selling pressure created earlier weakness, recent X sentiment and analyst targets near $20–$21 reflect growing optimism on long-term tailwinds.
sources: 15+ web news articles (Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, Motley Fool, TipRanks, company releases), 20+ recent X posts (semantic and keyword searches since early 2026)
